_note: This file is a design-time snapshot and may be out of date. For current step state, run: iw item-status <ID> --json. The DB is the authoritative source of truth (CR-00023).
title: LLM-as-judge test review (spike) — a stronger model scores newly-written tests against an assertion-strength rubric; advisory-only signal in the CodeReview step

steps.description: Labelled set + judge script + Makefile target + calibration run. Hand-pick 30-50 real tests from the iw-ai-core suite (half STRONG, half MEDIUM/WEAK), tagged with rationale, into tests/llm_judge/labelled_set.jsonl. Write scripts/llm_judge_test_review.py as a standalone utility (no orch.config dependency) that takes --test-file + --test-name, sends test+prod code to Claude Opus 4.7 (model id from projects.toml [projects.iw-ai-core.ai_assistant].default_model), and emits per-test JSON {file, test_name, scores: {assertion_specificity, behaviour_vs_mock, edge_coverage} on 1-5 scale, overall, rationale}. Read ANTHROPIC_API_KEY from os.environ; exit code 2 when missing (distinct from API failure = exit 1; success = 0). Log tokens=input/output and cost_usd to stderr on every invocation. No retry on judge failure. Add 'anthropic>=0.40,<1.0' to [dependency-groups] dev in pyproject.toml only if not already present; regenerate uv.lock. Add make llm-judge-calibrate target (thin wrapper over the script's --calibrate <jsonl> mode; aggregator logic lives in the script so it is unit-testable). Run the calibration: pipe make llm-judge-calibrate output to ai-dev/active/CR-00084/evidences/pre/cr-00084-judge-calibration.txt with header (date, model id, prices used). The evidence file must contain a confusion matrix (rows=true label, cols=predicted, both over {STRONG, MEDIUM, WEAK} with bucketing overall>=4 -> STRONG, ==3 -> MEDIUM, <=2 -> WEAK), WEAK-recall %, STRONG-FP %, Verdict: MET|NOT_MET (bar: WEAK-recall >= 70% AND STRONG-FP <= 30%), and total token spend in USD (budget < $2.00; do NOT auto-abort on overrun, just record it). TDD: write tests/unit/test_llm_judge_script.py FIRST (13 cases: validator happy/missing-scores/non-int/out-of-range/missing-rationale, loader happy/bad-label/missing-key, aggregator confusion-matrix/recall-fp-rate/skipped, argparse empty-name, exit-2-when-no-API-key), run targeted, capture RED, then implement. If ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is unavailable in the worktree, ship infrastructure with calibration_verdict: DEFERRED, raise a blocker note, and S02 ships the hook dormant. Record calibration_verdict (MET|NOT_MET|DEFERRED), calibration_cost_usd, labelled_set_size, labelled_set_strong_count, labelled_set_weak_count in the step report's structured fields - S02 reads calibration_verdict to decide hook form.

Agent-spec advisory hook in CodeReview, conditional on S01's calibration_verdict. Read S01's report; record the verdict (MET|NOT_MET|DEFERRED) in your own report's notes. Edit agents/claude/code-review-impl.md and agents/opencode/code-review-impl.md to add a new section '### 6. (Advisory) LLM-as-judge test-quality signal' after '### 5. Produce Findings' and before '## Severity Levels'. If MET: write the LIVE form with (a) optional invocation on each newly added test file in files_changed, picking 1-3 behaviour-heavy functions per file; (b) append judge JSON verbatim to the review report under '## Advisory: LLM-judge scores' subsection; (c) explicit prohibition: 'MUST NOT raise verdict to fail and MUST NOT increment mandatory_fix_count'; (d) graceful skip with one-line note when ANTHROPIC_API_KEY missing; (e) no retries (one-line log on failure); (f) per-review cumulative cost cap < $0.50; (g) forward link to ai-dev/active/CR-00084/evidences/pre/cr-00084-judge-calibration.txt. If NOT_MET or DEFERRED: write the DORMANT form with (a) note that the judge utility exists but calibration bar was not met; (b) explicit 'DO NOT invoke the judge in this review' instruction; (c) forward link to the same evidence file; (d) one-line re-enable path ('file a small follow-up CR that re-runs make llm-judge-calibrate and flips this section to the LIVE form'). Sync mirrors with iw sync-agents if present, otherwise cp directly; verify byte-identity with diff -q. Do NOT touch agents/pi/code-review-impl.md (out of scope) or any other agent spec. tdd_red_evidence: 'n/a - agent-spec markdown edits only, no production logic'.

Docs + skill + tracker sync. Read S01's calibration_verdict and the evidence file. (1) docs/IW_AI_Core_Testing_Strategy.md: add a new subsection (under §10 or as a new § directly after §10 - pick what fits the doc structure) covering the rubric (3 axes, 1-5 scale, bucketing rule), the calibration outcome (verdict + WEAK-recall + STRONG-FP + labelled-set size + total cost - quote numbers from the evidence file), the current disposition (LIVE in agents/{claude,opencode}/code-review-impl.md §6 OR DORMANT pending re-calibration), cost discipline (< $2.00 calibration budget, < $0.50 per-review cap, no retry/no auto-loop), and what's out of scope (promoting to blocking gate). Add one new entry to the doc's bottom changelog dated 2026-05-24. (2) skills/iw-ai-core-testing/SKILL.md: add a new '## Advisory: LLM-as-judge signal (CR-00084)' subsection mirroring the strategy doc but keyed to test-writer/reviewer audience. (3) Run uv run iw sync-skills --force iw-ai-core-testing; verify diff -q skills/iw-ai-core-testing/SKILL.md .claude/skills/iw-ai-core-testing/SKILL.md is empty. (4) ai-dev/work/TESTS_ENHANCEMENT.md: update §8 row 4.4 Status column from TODO to 'DONE (CR-00084, 2026-05-24)' if verdict==MET or 'DEFERRED (CR-00084, 2026-05-24)' if NOT_MET/DEFERRED, set Reference column to CR-00084, populate Notes column with one-line summary + link to evidence file if DEFERRED; append one sentence to the v1.3 header status block (around line 8) mentioning CR-00084 + verdict; add a new §11 changelog entry dated 2026-05-24 summarising labelled set (size + STRONG/WEAK split), judge script, verdict + recall/FP percentages, total cost vs budget, hook form, and an inline 'complementary to CR-00046's structural scanner' note. Do NOT touch tests/CLAUDE.md, skills/iw-workflow/SKILL.md, any other doc under docs/, or any other tracker section. tdd_red_evidence: 'n/a - docs/skill/tracker markdown edits only, no production logic'.

Per-step review of S01 + S02 + S03 in a single pass (S02 and S03 are conditional on S01 so reviewing together is cleaner). Verify S01: labelled set (30-50 records, valid JSON, valid labels, half STRONG ±20%, real tests, none on assertion_free_baseline.txt, rationale justifies label on 3-record spot-check), judge script (standalone - no orch imports, exit 2 verified by `unset ANTHROPIC_API_KEY; ... ; echo $?`, validator raises ValueError, prices hardcoded with drift comment, --calibrate mode present, no retry: grep retry|attempt|tenacity|backoff returns nothing or only comments), unit tests (all 13 cases present, no live API call, targeted run passes, tdd_red_evidence plausible), Makefile target (PHONY + recipe), calibration evidence file (header + matrix + recall + FP + Verdict line + cost matches S01's calibration_cost_usd). Verify S02: hook_form matches S01's calibration_verdict (CRITICAL if they disagree), LIVE form has all 5 mandatory clauses including the explicit 'MUST NOT raise verdict to fail' language, DORMANT form has forward link + DO NOT invoke + re-enable path, opencode mirror differs only in YAML frontmatter, both .claude/agents and .opencode/agents mirrors byte-identical via diff -q, agents/pi/code-review-impl.md unchanged via git diff main. Verify S03: strategy doc subsection cross-references evidence file numbers exactly, skill mirror byte-identical, tracker row 4.4 status matches verdict, §11 changelog entry has all required fields, header sentence added. Scope: git diff --name-only main..HEAD only shows files in allowed_paths; ZERO files under orch/ dashboard/ executor/; pyproject.toml + uv.lock only if S01 added the anthropic dep. Pre-review gate: make lint + make format show zero new violations on changed files. Targeted test run: uv run pytest tests/unit/test_llm_judge_script.py -v.

Global cross-step review covering AC1-AC7. AC1: tests/llm_judge/labelled_set.jsonl has 30-50 records, valid JSON, half STRONG (±20%). AC2: judge script CLI works, JSON shape correct, exit-code 2 verified by direct invocation with key unset. AC3: calibration evidence file present with confusion matrix + WEAK-recall + STRONG-FP + MET|NOT_MET verdict + total cost < $2.00 (or explicit overrun acknowledgement). AC4: advisory hook form (LIVE vs DORMANT) in agents/{claude,opencode}/code-review-impl.md matches calibration verdict from S01; .claude/agents/ and .opencode/agents/ mirrors byte-identical to masters. AC5: git diff --name-only main..HEAD shows ZERO files under orch/ dashboard/ executor/. AC6: docs (strategy + skill) and tracker (row 4.4 + §11) all quote the same verdict + percentages from the evidence file. AC7: pending S06-S13 (record as PENDING_S06_S13). The headline cross-cutting check is the calibration -> hook form -> docs disposition chain: read all 6 surfaces (evidence file Verdict line, agent spec LIVE/DORMANT body, strategy doc current-disposition sentence, skill LIVE/DORMANT note, tracker row 4.4 DONE/DEFERRED, tracker §11 outcome summary) and confirm one consistent story - any inconsistency is CRITICAL. Cost discipline (cross-cutting): S01's calibration_cost_usd < $2.00 (or explicit note), LIVE form (if applicable) declares < $0.50 per-review cap, no automatic retry anywhere. Advisory-contract safety: confirm the LIVE form body explicitly forbids judge-score -> verdict:fail; weak wording is HIGH. Per-AC verdict dictionary mandatory in result contract. Run make test-unit (full unit suite for regression). Do NOT run make test-integration (S10 owns it) or make llm-judge-calibrate (S01's evidence is the source of truth - rerunning would burn the budget twice).
QV: Type checking (the new judge script must type-check cleanly under mypy)
QV: Unit tests (includes the new tests/unit/test_llm_judge_script.py)
QV: Integration tests (no new integration tests in this CR; gate runs the existing suite to catch any regression from the doc/skill/agent-spec edits)

QV: Assertion scanner (CR-00046's structural gate). This CR's new tests/unit/test_llm_judge_script.py must not add any no-assert/tautology/mock-only/broad-raises violations beyond the existing baseline.
QV: Secret scanning (gitleaks). ANTHROPIC_API_KEY is read from environment only - no hardcoded key anywhere in the judge script or tests.

Self-assessment of the just-completed item via the iw-item-analyze skill. Spike-specific focus: (a) did calibration_verdict in S01 propagate correctly to S02's hook_form and S03's tracker_row_4_4_status (and did per-step or final review catch any mismatch); (b) cost discipline - S01 under $2 budget, S02 declares per-review cap if LIVE; (c) advisory-only safety held under pressure - no wording in agent spec lets the judge score raise verdict to fail; (d) S01 tdd_red_evidence is a real RED run (AssertionError/AttributeError on undefined _validate_judge_payload etc.), not a placeholder - compare against CR-00045 TDD-evidence patterns; (e) compare overall spike patterns against CR-00059's mutmut spike (the closest precedent for 'spike with calibration step that decides disposition'); (f) any QV gate (S06-S13) burned a fix cycle and why - common culprits are lint on the new script, JSONL formatting on the labelled set, assertion-scanner picking up the new unit tests; (g) labelled set defensibility - none of the 30-50 labelled tests overlap with assertion_free_baseline.txt (HIGH finding if they do, but soft-step so does NOT block).
